oligos.name: C1-P1-T31
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer (anchored oligo-dT) carrying the Illumina P1/read-1 (P5) sequence and a PvuI site
oligos.notes: 5'-biotinylated (Bio-). P1/P5 sequence (AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT) + CG + 31-nt oligo(dT). The ...CCGATCG... embeds a PvuI recognition site (CGATCG) so that oligo-dT-derived 3' fragments can be cleaved and removed during library isolation. Used at 4 uM in the lysis buffer.

oligos.name: C1-P1-RNA-TSO
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo carrying the Illumina P1/read-1 adapter + 5 bp UMI + rGrGrG
oligos.notes: 5'-biotinylated all-RNA oligo (rNTPs; raw form Bio-AAUGAUACGGCGACCACCGAUNNNNNGGG, folded to DNA letters here). 29 nt total = P1 adapter (21) + 5 bp random UMI (NNNNN) + terminal GGG that pairs with the 3' dCdCdC overhang added by the reverse transcriptase at the transcript 5' cap. Being all-RNA, it hydrolyses during the hot-start step so it cannot act as a PCR primer. Used at 1.8 uM.

oligos.name: C1-P1-PCR-2
oligos.role: PCR amplification primer and Read-1 sequencing primer
oligos.notes: 5'-biotinylated (Bio-). Anneals to the shared P1 sequence present at both ends of the cDNA after RT; used at 530 nM for the 21-cycle PCR and also as the read-1 sequencing primer on the HiSeq 2000.

oligos.name: C1-TN5-U
oligos.role: Universal Tn5 mosaic-end (ME) transposon strand and index-read sequencing primer
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated (PHO-). This is the non-transferred mosaic-end strand; it is annealed with each barcoded C1-TN5-x strand to assemble the transposome. Also used as the index-read (barcode) sequencing primer.

oligos.name: C1-TN5-x (barcoded Tn5 adaptor, 96 variants)
oligos.role: Barcoded transferred Tn5 transposon strand carrying the Illumina P2/P7 sequence + 8 bp well (cell) barcode + mosaic end
oligos.notes: 96 well-specific variants (C1-TN5-1 ... C1-TN5-96). Structure: P7 (CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGA) + 8 bp barcode + reverse-complement of the mosaic end (GCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG, whose ME core is 5'-AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'). Example barcodes: 1=CGTCTAAT, 2=AGACTCGT, 3=GCACGTCA, 4=TCAACGAC, 5=ATTTAGCG ... 92=TAAGGTCC, 93=GAAAGGTG, 94=ACGTTGTA, 95=GCAGAGAA, 96=GCATTTGG. Each is annealed with C1-TN5-U and loaded with 6.25 uM Tn5 to make a well-specific transposome.

oligos.name: C1-TN5 barcoded transposome adaptor (annealed)
oligos.role: Double-stranded Tn5 adaptor loaded onto Tn5 transposase for tagmentation

components.name: C1-TN5-x (transferred strand)
components.role: Transferred strand: P7 + 8 bp well barcode + mosaic end; its 3'-OH ME end is joined to target DNA

components.name: C1-TN5-U (non-transferred strand)
components.role: 5'-phosphorylated non-transferred mosaic-end strand; anneals to the ME portion of C1-TN5-x

oligos.notes: The 19 bp mosaic-end duplex (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG / CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT) forms between the 3' end of C1-TN5-x and C1-TN5-U. 96 barcode-specific transposomes were prepared, one per well.

final_library.source_label: STRT-seq-C1 final Illumina library (5'-most fragment; top strand 5'->3')
annotation_lines: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT  = P1 / Illumina read-1 adapter (P5), contributed by the template-switching oligo (5'-biotin end)
[UMI:5]                = 5 bp random unique molecular identifier (from the TSO)
[CDNA]                 = 5' end of the transcript (sense), retained 5'-most fragment
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGC = reverse complement of the Tn5 mosaic end (added by tagmentation)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]       = well-specific cell barcode (top strand shows reverse complement of the printed barcode)

evidence.locator: STRT-seq-C1 final Illumina library (5'-most fragment; top strand 5'->3')

library_generation.title: Single-cell capture (Fluidigm C1)
library_generation.summary: One cell is captured in each of up to 96 microfluidic chambers on a C1 AutoPrep IFC chip and imaged.
library_generation.note: A ~12,000-cell suspension is loaded; the 'mRNA Seq: Cell Load' script isolates single cells (10-17 um chip) and each site is imaged for QC.
library_generation.product: [ C1 chamber: 1 single cell ]  x up to 96 wells

library_generation.title: Lysis, RT + template switching
library_generation.summary: Cells are lysed and reverse-transcribed with oligo(dT) primer C1-P1-T31 while the all-RNA TSO C1-P1-RNA-TSO adds P1+UMI+GGG to the transcript 5' end.
library_generation.note: SuperScript II extends from the oligo(dT), reaches the 5' cap, adds a CCC overhang, and template-switches onto the GGG of the TSO, tagging each molecule with a 5 bp UMI and the Illumina read-1 adapter.
library_generation.product:
mRNA         5'- [cap] XXXXXXXXXXXX              AAAA...A -3'
                       ||||||||||||              ||||||||
1st cDNA 3'- GGG NNNNN TAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA ... TTTT...T GCTAGCC... -5'

full-length cDNA (sense, after switching):
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT NNNNN GGG [CDNA] AAAA...A CGATCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'

library_generation.title: PCR amplification (21 cycles)
library_generation.summary: Diluted (1:10) cDNA is amplified with a single primer C1-P1-PCR-2 that binds the shared P1 end at both termini.
library_generation.note: Because both ends carry the P1 sequence, one primer suffices; 21 cycles give ~1 ng/uL amplified cDNA. The all-RNA TSO has hydrolysed and cannot prime.
library_generation.product:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT NNNNN GGG [CDNA] AAAA...A CGATCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTA NNNNN CCC [CDNA] TTTT...T GCTAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA -5'

library_generation.title: Tagmentation with barcoded Tn5
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is fragmented and tagged in one step by well-specific Tn5 transposomes carrying P7 + 8 bp barcode + mosaic end.
library_generation.note: Each of 96 wells uses a distinct barcoded transposome (C1-TN5-x annealed to C1-TN5-U); the mosaic end is inserted internally, adding the read-2/P7 adapter and cell barcode.
library_generation.product:
5'- AATGA...CCGAT NNNNN GGG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGC [BC-rc] TCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- TTACT...GGCTA NNNNN CCC [CDNA] GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCG [BC]    AGCATACGGCAGAAGACGGAAC -5'

library_generation.title: Streptavidin capture of terminal fragments
library_generation.summary: Biotinylated P1 ends are captured on MyOne streptavidin beads, discarding all internal (non-biotin) fragments.
library_generation.note: Only the 5'-most and 3'-most fragments carry the biotinylated P1 adapter and are retained on the beads; internal fragments are washed away.
library_generation.product:
[bead]~Bio-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT NNNNN GGG [CDNA] ...ME...[BC]...P7   (5' fragment)
[bead]~Bio-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGATCG TTTT...A [CDNA] ...ME...[BC]...P7   (3' fragment, oligo-dT end)

library_generation.title: PvuI digestion (remove 3' fragments)
library_generation.summary: PvuI-HF cleaves the CGATCG site present only in the oligo(dT)-derived 3' fragments, releasing them.
library_generation.note: The T31 primer end contains ...CCGATCG... (PvuI); the TSO 5' end does not, so only the desired 5'-most fragments remain on the beads.
library_generation.product:
retained 5' fragment:
[bead]~Bio-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT NNNNN GGG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGC [BC-rc] TCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG

library_generation.title: Alkaline elution of single-stranded library
library_generation.summary: The single-stranded 5' library is eluted from the beads with NaOH and neutralised, ready for sequencing.
library_generation.note: Yield ~1-5 nM ssDNA library, pooled across the 96 wells and quantified by qPCR (KAPA Library Quant).

library_generation.title: Optional 9-cycle library PCR (187ds only)
library_generation.summary: The pooled ss library may optionally be amplified nine additional cycles (used only for the 187ds sample; not recommended).
library_generation.note: Most samples (187ss) were sequenced directly without further amplification; this step is optional.
library_generation.product: double-stranded amplified pooled library (same structure as step 7)

library_sequencing.note: Primes on the P1/read-1 region and reads the 5 bp UMI, the template-switch GGG, and extends into the 5' end of the transcript. Only reads with a valid barcode, a 5 bp UMI, and >=3 Gs at the template-switch site are kept.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer C1-P1-PCR-2 anneals to the bottom strand and reads UMI + GGG + transcript 5' end
primer  5'- GAATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT---------------------->
top     5'-  AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAT NNNNN GGG [cDNA]... -3'
             |||||||||||||||||||||
bottom  3'-  TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTA NNNNN CCC [cDNA]... -5'
read                               NNNNN GGG [cDNA]  (50 bp)

library_sequencing.note: Separate index read; the mosaic-end primer anneals adjacent to the tagmentation-added barcode and reads the well-specific 8 bp cell barcode.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index-read primer C1-TN5-U anneals to the bottom strand and reads the 8 bp well barcode
primer  5'- CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGC-------->
top     5'- CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGC NNNNNNNN TCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
            ||||||||||||||||||||||||          |||||||||||||||||||||
bottom  3'- GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCG NNNNNNNN AGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
read                                 NNNNNNNN  (8 bp barcode)

title: STRT-seq-C1
description: STRT-seq-C1 is a Fluidigm C1 microfluidic single-cell RNA-seq method that quantifies transcripts by their 5' ends using unique molecular identifiers (UMIs). During reverse transcription an oligo(dT) primer and an all-RNA template-switching oligo carrying a 5 bp random UMI append the Illumina read-1 adapter to the transcript 5' end; the cDNA is PCR-amplified and then tagmented with well-barcoded Tn5 transposomes that add the read-2/P7 adapter and an 8 bp well (cell) barcode. Streptavidin capture plus PvuI digestion retain only the 5'-most fragment. Read 1 (50 bp) reads the UMI, the template-switch GGG and the 5' end of the transcript, and a separate 8 bp index read reads the well barcode, enabling absolute molecule counting per cell.

original_publication.title: Quantitative single-cell RNA-seq with unique molecular identifiers

authors.name: Saiful Islam

throughput.summary: Up to 96 single cells captured and barcoded per Fluidigm C1 run; 48% mRNA capture efficiency; average estimated total mRNA content 200,739 molecules per ES cell.
throughput.cells: Up to 96 single cells per C1 run (one cell per capture chamber)
throughput.rna: 48 ± 5% RNA capture efficiency (from ERCC spike-ins); ~1.54 million mapped reads/cell; average 9 reads per molecule
throughput.dna: ~1 ng/uL amplified cDNA per cell; 1-5 nM single-stranded library after isolation

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (molecule counts modeled as Poisson; a lossy Poisson with loss factor f=0.20 fit to ERCC controls for noise detection; UMI counts corrected for UMI collision probability)

other.label: PCR cycles
other.value: 21 cycles (down from 33 in original STRT); no separate library PCR

other.label: TSO change
other.value: Shortened to 29 bp all-RNA TSO with no well barcode, containing the Illumina read-1 adapter

other.label: Sequencer
other.value: Illumina HiSeq 2000; 50 bp read 1 + 8 bp index read

data_processing.summary: Reads are quality-filtered, the well barcode and 5 bp UMI are extracted, template-switch Gs and poly(A) tails trimmed, then aligned with Bowtie to the genome plus an ERCC/splice-junction artificial chromosome; reads are assigned to UCSC transcript models and molecules counted as distinct UMIs per position (with collision correction) to yield an absolute per-cell expression matrix.
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson (lossy Poisson with loss factor f=0.20 fit to ERCC spike-in controls; UMI counts corrected for collision probability)
